appName: Browser extension name shown in store listings, the extension list, and the browser action title; also used in manifest metadata.
appDescription: Extension description shown in browser store listings and the extension list; also used in manifest metadata.
appActionTitle: Tooltip shown when hovering the extension icon in the browser toolbar; also used in manifest metadata.
icon: Form field label for selecting an icon in widget configuration screens.
title: Form field label for entering a widget's title or display name in configuration screens.
url: Form field label for entering a URL in widget configuration screens.
urls: Form field label for a list of web addresses in widget configuration screens.
save: Button label that saves the current widget configuration form.
import: Button label that imports a value from an external source (such as browser bookmarks) into a form field.
add: Button label for adding a new item to a list in widget configuration screens.
noResults: Empty-state message displayed when a search or filter produces no matching items.
example: Placeholder title text used in example widget previews (iframe and bookmark previews).
timezone: Form field label for selecting a timezone in the date/time widget configuration screen.
refreshing: Transient status text shown while RSS feeds are being refreshed.
refresh: Button to refresh/reload content in widgets such as RSS feeds.
startTypingToSearch: Placeholder text inside dropdown/combobox search inputs prompting the user to start typing.
search: Placeholder text inside search input fields for finding icons or widgets.
loading: Generic loading status shown while fetching data (weather/RSS widgets, cloud profile list).
lastUpdatedAt: Timestamp label showing when a data source was last refreshed (weather, RSS).
home: Name of the default folder where widgets are placed by default; also appears in onboarding text about folders.
availableOnlyInEnglish: Notice shown in the 'What's new' modal when the browser language is not English, indicating release notes are English-only.
px: Unit suffix (pixels) appended to numeric size/breakpoint inputs.
whatsNew: Sidebar button label and modal title for the release notes / changelog feature.
editWidget: Modal dialog title for editing the configuration of an already-placed widget.
configureWidget: Modal dialog title for configuring a newly added widget during the add-widget wizard.
addWidget: Button/modal title for selecting and adding a new widget to the current folder; also a step in onboarding.
editFolder: Sidebar icon-button tooltip that toggles edit mode for the currently open folder, letting the user rearrange, resize, add, and remove widgets.
addWidgetConfigInvalid: Error alert shown in the add-widget wizard when the widget's configuration fails validation and can't be saved. 'console' refers to the browser developer console.
saveWidgetConfigInvalid: Error alert shown in the edit-widget modal when the edited configuration fails validation and can't be saved. 'console' refers to the browser developer console.
next: Navigation button advancing to the next step of the onboarding wizard.
back: Navigation button returning to the previous step of a wizard or dialog.
icons: Heading for the section listing available built-in icons in the icon picker.
customIcons: Option/tab label in the icon picker for the user's own uploaded icon set.
canBeEmpty: Inline parenthetical hint next to an optional form field label, indicating it may be left blank.

cloud.connect: Button to connect the current browser to a cloud sync profile (sidebar and profile cards).
cloud.connected: Badge next to a profile name indicating it is currently connected and syncing.
cloud.disconnect: Button to disconnect the current browser from the active cloud profile.
cloud.cancel: Button to dismiss cloud modals (profile creation, rename, connect confirmation).
cloud.account: Cloud account modal title and the sidebar button that opens it.
cloud.login: Submit button on the cloud login form.
cloud.logout: Button to log out of the cloud account in the connected-account view.
cloud.email: Email input field on the cloud login form.
cloud.password: Password input field on the cloud login form.
cloud.connectedAs: Label showing which email the user is logged in as.
cloud.noAccount: Prompt text shown before the sign-up link on the login form.
cloud.createAccount: Link on the login form that takes the user to create an account.

userDataConflict.description: Shown during login when this device holds synced data (e.g. stash) that belongs to a different account than the one logging in. The user must decide before login completes.
userDataConflict.upload: Button that keeps the local data and uploads (merges) it into the account being logged into.
userDataConflict.discard: Button that deletes the local copy and uses the account's cloud data instead.

cloud.plusDescription: Description of the Anori Plus cloud-sync service shown to logged-out users.
cloud.learnMore: Link on the login screen to learn more about Anori Plus.
cloud.profiles: Heading for the list of sync profiles in the connected-account view.
cloud.noProfiles: Empty-state message when the user has no sync profiles yet.
cloud.createProfile: Button that opens the new-profile creation flow.
cloud.profileName: Input field for the new profile's name.
cloud.createdAt: Profile metadata label showing when the profile was created.
cloud.lastSyncedAt: Profile metadata label showing when the profile was last used.
cloud.create: Button that confirms creation of the new profile.
cloud.profileCreatedSuccess: Success message shown after a sync profile is created.
cloud.confirm: Generic confirm button in cloud confirmation dialogs (connect, delete).
cloud.rename: Button to rename an existing sync profile.
cloud.delete: Button to delete a sync profile.
cloud.deleteConfirmation: Confirmation prompt before deleting a profile, including the profile's name.

connectConfirmation.message: Confirmation prompt warning that connecting will overwrite the local configuration, including the profile's name.

error.invalidCredentials: Login error shown when the email or password is wrong.
error.unknown: Generic fallback error message for failed cloud requests.
error.failedToLoadProfiles: Error shown when the profile list fails to load.
error.profileNameRequired: Validation error when creating a profile without a name.
error.failedToCreateProfile: Error shown when profile creation fails.

iconsPicker.customIconsInfo: Informational text in the icon picker explaining what custom icons are.
iconsPicker.customIconsAbsent: Empty-state in the icon picker's custom-icons view prompting the user to upload one in settings.
iconsPicker.iconFamily: Label for the dropdown that selects an icon family in the icon picker.
iconsPicker.allIcons: Option in the icon-family dropdown showing icons from all families.
iconsPicker.selectFamilyOrSearch: Hint in the icon picker prompting the user to pick a family or search.

settings.title: Sidebar button that opens the Settings modal.

general.title: Title of the General tab in Settings.
general.language: Label for the interface language dropdown in General settings.
general.translationInfo: Note in General settings explaining community translations and linking to contribution instructions.
general.sidebarOrientation: Label for the sidebar orientation dropdown in General settings.
general.autoHideSidebar: Toggle label in General settings for auto-hiding the sidebar.
general.newTabTitle: Label for the input that customizes the browser tab title.
general.enableAnalytics: Toggle to enable sending analytics, in General settings and onboarding.
general.analyticsHintP1: First paragraph of the hint explaining what analytics collect, below the analytics toggle.
general.analyticsHintP2: Second paragraph of the analytics hint, with a 'Learn more' link.
general.showBookmarksBar: Toggle label in General settings for showing the bookmarks bar.
general.automaticCompact: Toggle label for switching to compact mode based on screen size.
general.automaticCompactModeThreshold: Label for the dropdown choosing the screen-width threshold for compact mode.
general.automaticCompactModeThresholdHint: Hint below the threshold dropdown showing the current screen width.
general.useCompact: Toggle label to always use compact mode.
general.showAnimationOnOpen: Toggle label for showing the open animation.
general.showAnimationOnOpenHint: Hint explaining the open animation, below its toggle.
general.rememberLastFolder: Toggle label for reopening the last open folder.

customIcons.title: Title of the Custom icons tab in Settings.
customIcons.incorrectFormat: Error shown when uploaded files are an unsupported format.
customIcons.noIcons: Empty-state when the user has no custom icons yet.
customIcons.iconName: Input field for naming an uploaded custom icon.
customIcons.invalidNames: Error shown when trying to save icons that have invalid names.
customIcons.nameContainsInvalidChars: Validation error below an icon-name field for disallowed characters.
customIcons.saveIcons: Button to save uploaded custom icons.
customIcons.supportedFormats: Hint describing supported image formats and recommended size for custom icons.
customIcons.importIcons: Button that opens the file picker to upload custom icons.

folders.title: Title of the Folders tab in Settings.
folders.createNew: Button to create a new folder in Folders settings.
folders.defaultName: Default name given to a newly created folder.

pluginSettings.title: Title of the Plugin settings tab in Settings.

theme.title: Title of the Theme tab in Settings.
theme.createCustom: Button to create a new custom theme.
theme.selectBackground: Button to choose a background image for a custom theme.
theme.blur: Label for the background blur slider in the custom theme editor.
theme.colorAccent: Label for the accent color picker in the custom theme editor.
theme.colorBackground: Label for the background color picker in the custom theme editor.
theme.colorText: Label for the text color picker in the custom theme editor.
theme.colorCode: Label for the hex color code input in the color picker.

importExport.title: Title of the Import & export tab in Settings.
importExport.info: Intro text on the Import & export screen describing backup/restore.
importExport.import: Button to import a backup file on the Import & export screen.
importExport.export: Button to export the current configuration as a backup file.
importExport.cloudWarning: Confirmation shown when importing while cloud sync is on, warning it will disconnect the sync profile.
importExport.cloudSyncHint: Note on the Import & export screen promoting Anori Plus, with a link to it.
importExport.importError: Error shown when a backup import fails, including the failure detail.

aboutHelp.title: Title of the Help & about tab in Settings.
aboutHelp.onlyEnglishPlease: Note shown to non-English users asking them to file issues in English.
aboutHelp.p1: Help & about paragraph stating Anori is free and open source, with links to the GitHub repo and to filing a new issue.
aboutHelp.p2: Help & about paragraph about modifying/extending the extension, with a link to the readme.
aboutHelp.p3: Help & about paragraph linking to the author's Twitter and a 'support Ukraine' page.
aboutHelp.p4: Help & about paragraph about supporting development financially, with a donation link.
aboutHelp.license: Heading above the license text in Help & about.

shortcuts.title: Heading for the keyboard-shortcuts cheatsheet (also a section in Help & about).
shortcuts.showCheatsheet: Cheatsheet description for the shortcut that toggles the shortcuts cheatsheet.
shortcuts.toggleSettings: Cheatsheet description for the shortcut that toggles the settings panel.
shortcuts.closeMenuOrModal: Cheatsheet description for the shortcut that closes the open modal or menu.
shortcuts.switchToFolderAbove: Cheatsheet description for the shortcut that switches to the folder above.
shortcuts.switchToFolderBelow: Cheatsheet description for the shortcut that switches to the folder below.
shortcuts.switchToNFolder: Cheatsheet description for the shortcut that switches to a folder by its number.
shortcuts.editCurrentFolder: Cheatsheet description for the shortcut that edits the current folder.
shortcuts.addNewWidget: Cheatsheet description for the shortcut that adds a new widget to the current folder.

start.title: Heading of the first onboarding step welcoming the user.
start.p1: Intro paragraph on the first onboarding step noting which languages the introduction is available in.
start.p2: Paragraph on the first onboarding step explaining the page is built from widgets.
start.p3: Paragraph on the first onboarding step explaining how to enter editing mode to add a widget.
start.p4: Paragraph on the first onboarding step explaining how to add a widget or exit editing mode.

folders.title: Heading of the onboarding step about folders.
folders.p1: Paragraph on the folders onboarding step explaining the default Home folder and organizing widgets into folders.
folders.p2: Paragraph on the folders onboarding step explaining how to open the folders manager in settings.

customization.title: Heading of the onboarding step about customization.
customization.p1: Paragraph on the customization onboarding step noting Anori can be customized further.
customization.p2: Paragraph on the customization onboarding step listing options like tab title, theme, background, and custom icons.
customization.p3: Paragraph on the customization onboarding step encouraging the user to explore settings.

analytics.title: Heading of the onboarding step about analytics.
analytics.p1: Paragraph on the analytics onboarding step explaining Anori collects no tracking data by default.
analytics.p2: Paragraph on the analytics onboarding step asking the user to opt in to anonymous usage analytics.
analytics.p3: Paragraph on the analytics onboarding step, with a link to details on what data is collected.

presets.title: Heading of the final onboarding step offering a ready-made setup.
presets.p2: Paragraph on the final onboarding step offering a prepared productive setup.
presets.p3: Paragraph on the final onboarding step noting the preset can be used as a starting point.
presets.cta: Button that applies the ready-made productive setup.

task.themes: Checklist item seeded into the starter setup, suggesting trying themes.
task.anoriPlus: Checklist item seeded into the starter setup, suggesting checking out Anori Plus.
task.widgets: Checklist item seeded into the starter setup, suggesting exploring widgets.

requirePermissions.eh: Short heading on the widget permission notice.
requirePermissions.modalTitle: Title of the dialog detailing the extra permissions a widget needs.
requirePermissions.compactText: Compact one-line notice that a widget needs extra permissions, clickable for details.
requirePermissions.text: Intro line before the list of permissions a widget requires.
requirePermissions.apiPermissions: Heading for the list of required browser API permissions.
requirePermissions.hostPermissions: Heading for the list of required host (website) permissions.
requirePermissions.grant: Button that grants the requested permissions.

bookmark-plugin.name: Bookmarks plugin name in the add-widget picker.
bookmark-plugin.widgetName: Name of the single Bookmark widget in the add-widget picker.
bookmark-plugin.groupWidgetName: Name of the Bookmark group widget in the add-widget picker.
bookmark-plugin.openInGroup: Toggle in the bookmark-group config to open bookmarks as a browser tab group.
bookmark-plugin.group: Label marking a widget as a bookmark group.
bookmark-plugin.searchBookmarks: Placeholder in the bookmark search input.
bookmark-plugin.checkStatus: Toggle in the bookmark config to enable periodic page up/down checks.
bookmark-plugin.checkStatusHint: Hint explaining how background page-status checking works.
bookmark-plugin.checkingStatus: Status shown while a bookmark's page status is being checked.
bookmark-plugin.status: Status line reporting whether the bookmarked page is up or down and when it was last checked.
bookmark-plugin.statusUp: Word inserted into the status line when the page is reachable.
bookmark-plugin.statusDown: Word inserted into the status line when the page is unreachable.
bookmark-plugin.permissionExplanation: Explanation shown when granting the permission needed for page-status checks (CORS).
bookmark-plugin.openInNewTab: Toggle in the bookmark config to open the bookmark in a new tab.

calendar-plugin.name: Calendar plugin name in the add-widget picker.
calendar-plugin.widgetName: Calendar widget name in the add-widget picker.
calendar-plugin.firstDayOfWeek: Label for the first-day-of-week setting in the Calendar widget config.

datetime-plugin.name: Date and time plugin name in the add-widget picker.
datetime-plugin.widgetNameS: Name of the small Date and time widget variant.
datetime-plugin.widgetNameM: Name of the medium Date and time widget variant.
datetime-plugin.withoutDate: Date-format option in the Date and time config for showing time without a date.
datetime-plugin.timeFormat: Label for the time-format setting in the Date and time config.
datetime-plugin.dateFormat: Label for the date-format setting in the Date and time config.

notes-plugin.name: Notes plugin/widget name in the add-widget picker.
notes-plugin.noteTitle: Placeholder for the note title field.
notes-plugin.noteText: Placeholder for the note body field, noting markdown is supported.
notes-plugin.exampleTitle: Demo note title shown in the Notes widget preview.
notes-plugin.exampleText: Demo note body shown in the Notes widget preview.

recently-closed-plugin.name: Recently closed tabs plugin name in the add-widget picker.
recently-closed-plugin.widgetTitle: Heading at the top of the Recently closed widget.
recently-closed-plugin.tab: Label marking a recently-closed entry as a tab.
recently-closed-plugin.window: Label marking a recently-closed entry as a window.

system-status-plugin.name: System status plugin name in the add-widget picker.
system-status-plugin.ramLoad: Name/label of the RAM load widget.
system-status-plugin.cpuLoad: Name/label of the CPU load widget.

tabs-plugin.name: Name of the tab-management plugin (holds the recently-closed widget) in the add-widget picker.

tasks-plugin.name: Tasks plugin name in the add-widget picker.
tasks-plugin.todo: Default title of the Tasks widget.
tasks-plugin.completeTask: Accessibility label for the button that marks a task complete.
tasks-plugin.taskDescription: Placeholder for the new-task input field.
tasks-plugin.noTasks: Empty-state shown when the task list is empty.
tasks-plugin.exampleTask0: Demo task shown in the Tasks widget preview.
tasks-plugin.exampleTask1: Demo task shown in the Tasks widget preview.
tasks-plugin.exampleTask2: Demo task shown in the Tasks widget preview.
tasks-plugin.exampleTask3: Demo task shown in the Tasks widget preview.

top-sites-plugin.name: Top sites plugin name in the add-widget picker.
top-sites-plugin.widgetHorizontal: Name of the horizontal Top sites widget variant.
top-sites-plugin.widgetVertical: Name of the vertical Top sites widget variant.

weather-plugin.name: Weather plugin name in the add-widget picker.
weather-plugin.weatherForecast: Name of the forecast Weather widget.
weather-plugin.forecast: Heading for the multi-day forecast in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.currentWeather: Name of the current-conditions Weather widget.
weather-plugin.kmh: Wind-speed unit abbreviation, kilometers per hour.
weather-plugin.ms: Wind-speed unit abbreviation, meters per second.
weather-plugin.mph: Wind-speed unit abbreviation, miles per hour.
weather-plugin.clearSky: Weather-condition label for clear sky in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.partlyCloudy: Weather-condition label for partly cloudy in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.fog: Weather-condition label for fog in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.drizzle: Weather-condition label for drizzle in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.freezingDrizzle: Weather-condition label for freezing drizzle in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.rain: Weather-condition label for rain in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.freezingRain: Weather-condition label for freezing rain in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.snow: Weather-condition label for snow in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.rainShower: Weather-condition label for rain showers in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.thunderstorm: Weather-condition label for thunderstorm in the Weather widget.
weather-plugin.unknownCode: Fallback condition text when the weather code is unrecognized.
weather-plugin.selectCity: Label/placeholder for the city selector in the Weather widget config.
weather-plugin.temperatureUnit: Label for the temperature-unit dropdown in the Weather widget config.
weather-plugin.speedUnit: Label for the wind-speed-unit dropdown in the Weather widget config.
weather-plugin.weatherCode: Tooltip showing the numeric weather code next to the condition text.
weather-plugin.attribution: Attribution line crediting the Open-Meteo data source, with a link.

rss-plugin.name: RSS plugin name in the add-widget picker.
rss-plugin.widgetFeedName: Name of the RSS feed widget (multiple posts).
rss-plugin.widgetLatestPostName: Name of the widget showing only the latest post from a feed.
rss-plugin.feedUrl: Label for the single RSS feed URL field.
rss-plugin.feedUrls: Label for the multiple RSS feed URLs field.
rss-plugin.noPosts: Empty-state shown when a feed has no posts yet.
rss-plugin.compactView: Toggle in the RSS config for a compact post layout.
rss-plugin.presetsTitle: Intro text in the RSS config inviting the user to add feeds or pick a preset.
rss-plugin.presetForDevelopers: Full label of the developer-focused feed preset.
rss-plugin.presetForDesigners: Full label of the designer-focused feed preset.
rss-plugin.presetInteresting: Full label of the general-interest feed preset.
rss-plugin.presetForDevelopersTitle: Short label for the developer feed preset.
rss-plugin.presetForDesignersTitle: Short label for the designer feed preset.
rss-plugin.presetInterestingTitle: Short label for the general-interest feed preset.
rss-plugin.examplePostTitle1: Demo post title shown in the RSS widget preview.
rss-plugin.examplePostDescription1: Demo post description shown in the RSS widget preview.
rss-plugin.exampleFeedTitle1: Demo feed title shown in the RSS widget preview.
rss-plugin.examplePostTitle2: Demo post title shown in the RSS widget preview.
rss-plugin.examplePostDescription2: Demo post description shown in the RSS widget preview.
rss-plugin.exampleFeedTitle2: Demo feed title shown in the RSS widget preview.
rss-plugin.examplePostTitle3: Demo post title shown in the RSS widget preview.
rss-plugin.examplePostDescription3: Demo post description shown in the RSS widget preview.
rss-plugin.exampleFeedTitle3: Demo feed title shown in the RSS widget preview.

iframe-plugin.name: Embed webpage plugin name in the add-widget picker.
iframe-plugin.expandWidgetName: Name of the expandable Embed webpage widget variant.
iframe-plugin.showLink: Toggle in the embed config to show a link to the original page.
iframe-plugin.limitations: Notice in the embed config warning that some pages block being embedded.

math-plugin.name: Math plugin name in the add-widget picker.
math-plugin.expandWidgetName: Name of the expandable calculator widget variant.
math-plugin.cantCalc: Message shown when the calculator can't evaluate the expression.

picture-plugin.name: Picture plugin name in the add-widget picker.
picture-plugin.imageSource: Label for the image-source selector (URL or local file) in the Picture config.
picture-plugin.localFile: Option for using a local file as the image source.
picture-plugin.image: Label for the local image field in the Picture config.
picture-plugin.selectImage: Button to pick a local image when none is selected.
picture-plugin.changeImage: Button to replace the currently selected local image.

anki-plugin.name: Anki plugin name in the add-widget picker.
anki-plugin.deck: Label for the deck selector in the Anki widget config.
anki-plugin.showAnswer: Button that reveals the answer during flashcard review.
anki-plugin.easy: Answer-grade button rating a reviewed card as easy.
anki-plugin.again: Answer-grade button marking a reviewed card to be shown again.
anki-plugin.good: Answer-grade button rating a reviewed card as good.
anki-plugin.hard: Answer-grade button rating a reviewed card as hard.
anki-plugin.error: Error shown when the widget can't reach AnkiConnect (Anki not running or addon missing).
anki-plugin.widgetName: Anki widget name in the add-widget picker.
